content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
April 15,
1992
D.
R. Fields
Treasurer
Committee to Elect
John Gordon
2220 country Club Drive
Glendora,
CA
91740
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-92-204
Dear Mr. Fields:
This is in response to your request for confirmation of
telephone advice provided to you on March 12,
1992, regarding the
appropriate use of campaign funds and the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”) .
This letter
confirms that your letter dated March 31,
1992 accurately
summarizes the advice I provided to you on March 12,
1992.
As I stated in our telephone conversation,
a candidate may
contribute unlimited amounts of personal funds to his campaign
committee so long as the funds are deposited in and expended from
the campaign bank account.
Moreover, the general rule of the personal use provisions of
the Act is that any expenditure of campaign funds must be, at a
minimum, reasonably related to a politica., legislative, or
governmental purpose.
2
(Section 89512.)
Consequently,
so long as
the repayment of the campaign contributions to contributors is
reasonably related to a political purpose, the expenditure is
permitted.
(See,
Dorinan Advice Letter,
No.
1-91-253.)
We have said that the pro—rata return of contributions was a
reasonable use of campaign funds.
(Goodwin Advice Letter,
No.
A—
90-571.)
By the same rationale, the total repayment of all
campaign contributions received is also permissible.
In addition,
1
Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et q.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660


Our File No. A-92-204
Page 2
the failure to repay the candidate himself does not affect the
reasonableness of the expenditures.
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at
(916)
322-59O1.
Sincerely,
SH:JWW: aa
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
rohn W. Wallace
Legal Division
2
Expenditures which confer a substantial personal benefit must
be directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental
purpose.
(Section 89512.)
Additionally,
some expenditures must
meet the “directly related” standard by statute.
Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 1Oc per page.

sections.conclusion: A candidate may contribute unlimited amounts of personal funds to his campaign committee so long as the funds are deposited in and expended from the campaign bank account. The repayment of campaign contributions to contributors is permissible so long as it is reasonably related to a political purpose, and the pro-rata return or total repayment of all campaign contributions received is also permissible. The failure to repay the candidate himself does not affect the reasonableness of the expenditures.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a candidate may contribute unlimited personal funds to their campaign committee and whether the repayment of campaign contributions to contributors constitutes a permissible use of campaign funds under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirms that candidates may contribute unlimited personal funds to their campaign committees and that repaying campaign contributions to donors is a permissible use of campaign funds as long as it is reasonably related to a political purpose. The failure to repay the candidate personally does not render such repayments impermissible.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a confirmation letter of prior telephone advice dated March 12, 1992. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers are present, but the substantive question and answer are clearly articulated in the body of the letter. The letter references prior FPPC advice letters (Dorman No. 1-91-253 and Goodwin No. A-90-571) as supporting authority.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a candidate may contribute unlimited personal funds to their campaign committee and whether the repayment of campaign contributions to contributors constitutes a permissible use of campaign funds under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: A candidate may contribute unlimited amounts of personal funds to his campaign committee so long as the funds are deposited in and expended from the campaign bank account. The repayment of campaign contributions to contributors is permissible so long as it is reasonably related to a political purpose, and the pro-rata return or total repayment of all campaign contributions received is also permissible. The failure to repay the candidate himself does not affect the reasonableness of the expenditures.

Moreover, the general rule of the personal use provisions of the Act is that any expenditure of campaign funds must be, at a minimum, reasonably related to a politica., legislative, or governmental purpose. 2 (Section 89512.) embedding.summary: This FPPC advice letter confirms telephone advice regarding a candidate's ability to contribute unlimited personal funds to a campaign committee and clarifies that repaying campaign contributions to contributors is a permissible expenditure if reasonably related to a political purpose.
